pages.raw_text:
Special Correspondence to The Press


BOISE. May 2S.--Idaho is the
home of the wanderers. They have
searched the country, some of them
the world, for place good enough
for the establishment of home,
and have finally settled In this land
of promise.


Tests applied to men in the effort
to get a jury in the Haywood case
brings them to the surface. 1t beats
the census in turning up the his
tory of the population.


M. H. Goodwin, a tall. straight,
muscular old fellow, with gray hair
and beard and twinkling blue eyes,
was typical case.


Where was he born? In Maine.
Where was his next place of resi-
dence? Massachusetts. Where next?
Minnesota. Next? Mississippi.
Next? Maine. Well? California.
Then where? Oregon. Next?
Washington. Then? Idaho, and
here he has lived for 20 years. He
is in the lumber business. and a
solid citizen. He has ben farmer,
carpenter and miner. He was not q
southern sympathizer and so ex-
planned: 81 left Mississippi in
something of a hurry in 1861"


Another interesting case was that
of Capt. Yates. For 80 years he
sailed as a ship captain from New
England to all parts of the world
Here he is, up in the mountains
and president of a big national
bank.


A tall. bearded Missourian said he
had spent several months In an in
terror Texas town, and had spent
several other months In the state
on the way home to Missouri


Foreigners have not penetrated to
this region to any great extent.
With two exceptions every man OF
more than 100 examined for jury
duty was born In the states. Mis-
souri, Is in the lead as mother of
Idahoans, with Iowa good second.
Half the states wer represented.
There was one man from New York
and one from South Carolina. Maine
contributed half dozen or more
Many of them came here as gold
seekers and. failing to make a
strike, settled down to agriculture
and stock raising. Now they form
a high standard of citizenship. They
are men Of family, land owners. In
telligent and independent Amer
icsRs.


That suggests another striking
phase Of the great trial. 1t Is con
test between two classes Of Amer
lean citizens that will be decided
by q third class.


All the wealth and influence o
the mine owners have been brough


to bear In an effort to convict Hay


wood. and, through him, the West
Tern Federation Of Miners. The fec
